title: Two Paths of Representation
concept: BERT and GPT represent two opposing philosophies: bidirectional context extraction vs. causal autoregressive generation.

exercises.prompt: What is the main difference between the design of BERT and GPT?

options.text: How they read and represent text context
The programming language they are written in
The type of computer chips they run on

exercises.explain: BERT and GPT represent two different design choices: bidirectional context extraction (looking at both sides) vs. causal generation (looking forward).

cards.title: How AIs Read
cards.body: Have you ever wondered why some AIs are amazing at answering questions, while others are great at writing long stories? It all comes down to how they read: do they look at the whole sentence at once, or do they read strictly from left to right?

cards.title: Two Opposing Philosophies
cards.body: BERT uses a 'bidirectional' approach, meaning it looks at the words before AND after a specific word to understand its full context. GPT uses a 'causal' or 'autoregressive' approach, meaning it only looks at past words to predict the very next word in a sequence.

cards.title: The Detective vs. The Novelist
cards.body: Imagine a detective investigating a crime scene vs. a writer drafting a novel. BERT is the detective: they look at all the clues at the same time—both before and after the crime—to understand the whole picture. GPT is the writer: they write the story word-by-word, from left to right, only knowing what they have written so far.

cards.title: Decoding the Bank
cards.body: In the sentence 'The bank of the river was muddy', BERT looks at 'river' to figure out that 'bank' means land, not a place for money. In contrast, if GPT is given 'The bank of the...', it must guess the next word (like 'river' or 'lake') using only the words that came before it.

cards.title: Is GPT Always Better?
cards.body: You might think that because GPT is newer and famous for chatting, it is automatically better than BERT at everything. In reality, BERT is often much better at analyzing existing text, finding errors, or searching documents because it can look at the complete sentence structure at the same time.

cards.title: Side-by-Side Comparison
cards.body: Here is how the two architectures process information. One sees everything at once to analyze, while the other steps forward one word at a time to build.

right.text: Looks only at past words. Perfect for generating and writing new text.

left.text: Looks left and right simultaneously. Perfect for understanding and analyzing existing text.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary goal of GPT during its training process?

options.text: Predicting the next word in a sequence
Hiding words in the middle and guessing them
Translating entire books instantly without training

exercises.explain: GPT is built to predict the next word in a sequence, making it a generative, causal model.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A causal model like GPT is prevented from looking at future words when predicting the next word.
exercises.explain: Causal models like GPT are restricted to looking only at past words (left-to-right) so they can learn to generate text.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to describe BERT's reading style.
exercises.sentence: BERT uses a _______ context extraction method to look at the entire sentence at once.
exercises.explain: BERT looks at both left and right contexts simultaneously, which is called bidirectional representation.

exercises.prompt: Which of these AI profiles represents the causal autoregressive approach?

options.text: Profile A: An AI that looks at the surrounding text on both sides to classify sentiment.
Profile B: An AI that generates a poem word-by-word by predicting what comes next.
Profile C: An AI that highlights spelling mistakes by comparing words to a dictionary.

exercises.explain: Profile B describes GPT, which is a causal autoregressive model that generates text one step at a time.

exercises.prompt: If you had to explain the core difference between BERT and GPT to a friend, which statement is most accurate?

options.text: BERT only reads text from right to left, while GPT reads it from left to right.
BERT reads in both directions at once to understand text, while GPT reads left-to-right to generate new text.
BERT generates text word-by-word, while GPT can only translate text into different languages.
BERT and GPT use the exact same reading direction but have different vocabulary sizes.

exercises.explain: BERT looks at both sides of a word simultaneously (bidirectional) to grasp deep meaning, whereas GPT only looks at past words (causal) to predict what comes next. This makes BERT excellent at analyzing text and GPT excellent at writing it.
